name: Ramanathaswamy Temple
description: One of the 12 Jyotirlingas and Char Dham sites. Has the longest corridor of any Hindu temple. Famous for 22 sacred wells (theerthams) ritual bathing.

name: Dhanushkodi
description: Ghost town at India's southeastern tip destroyed by a 1964 cyclone. Where the Bay of Bengal meets the Indian Ocean. Hauntingly beautiful ruins.

name: Pamban Bridge
description: India's first and longest sea bridge connecting Rameshwaram island to mainland. The cantilever section opens for ships to pass through.

name: Agni Theertham
description: Sacred beach right outside the temple's east gate. Pilgrims bathe here before entering Ramanathaswamy Temple. Only sea-shore theertham.

name: Adam's Bridge (Ram Setu)
description: Chain of limestone shoals between India and Sri Lanka. Believed to be the bridge built by Lord Rama's army. Visible from Dhanushkodi tip.

name: Gandhamadhana Parvatham
description: Small hillock — the highest point on the island. Houses Lord Rama's footprint impression. Offers panoramic views of the entire island.

name: Kothandaramaswamy Temple
description: Temple where Vibhishana surrendered to Lord Rama. One of the few structures to survive the 1964 cyclone that destroyed Dhanushkodi.

name: Five-Faced Hanuman Temple
description: Temple housing a unique floating stone said to have been used by Rama's army to build the bridge. Hanuman shown in rare Panchamukhi form.

name: Thiruppullani
description: Ancient Divya Desam where Lord Rama is said to have rested on a bed of Darbha grass before building the bridge to Lanka.

name: Devipattinam
description: Ancient Navagraha temple with nine stone pillars submerged in the sea representing the nine planets. Unique tidal phenomenon.

name: Villoondi Tirtham
description: Fresh water spring emerging from the middle of the sea. Legend says Rama shot an arrow into the ground creating this spring for his thirsty army.

name: Jada Tirtham
description: Sacred freshwater spring where Lord Rama performed penance. Clear, clean water considered highly auspicious for rituals.

name: Nambu Nayagiamman Temple
description: Ancient temple dedicated to the local goddess Nambu Nayagi. One of the important subsidiary shrines visited during the temple pilgrimage.

name: Ariyaman Beach
description: Government-maintained beach with clear blue water, white sand, and water sports. Less crowded alternative to the main temple beach.

name: Lakshmana Tirtham
description: One of the 22 sacred theerthams within the Ramanathaswamy Temple complex. Pilgrims bathe in all 22 for spiritual purification.

name: Kunthu Kal
description: Scenic spot where you can see both Bay of Bengal and Indian Ocean. Clear shallow waters make it perfect for wading.

name: Annai Indira Gandhi Road Bridge
description: 2-km road bridge running parallel to the Pamban rail bridge. Offers stunning views of the sea and is the lifeline to the island.

name: Water Bird Sanctuary
description: Ramanathapuram bird sanctuary hosting migratory birds from Siberia and Central Asia during winter months.

name: Pamban Fishing Village
description: Colorful fishing village near the Pamban bridge. Watch traditional fishing boats and experience the local coastal Tamil culture.

name: Abdul Wahab Dargah
description: A symbol of communal harmony — this Muslim dargah inside a Hindu pilgrimage town is revered by both communities.
